title: Core Workerpool Integration
impact: Replace custom WorkerPool with mature workerpool library for fuzzy search parallelization

stepByStep: Wrap existing logic in a named function: function searchEntities(data: WorkerInput): MatchResult[]
Replace parentPort logic with: workerpool.worker({ searchEntities });
acceptanceCriteria: Worker exports searchEntities function via workerpool.worker()

stepByStep: Replace import { WorkerPool } from '../workers/WorkerPool.js'; with import workerpool from '@danielsimonjr/workerpool';
Change type: private workerPool: workerpool.Pool | null = null;
if (!this.workerPool) { this.workerPool = workerpool.pool(this.workerPath, { maxWorkers: workerpool.cpus - 1, workerType: 'thread' }); }
const results = await Promise.all(chunks.map(chunk => this.workerPool!.exec('searchEntities', [{ query, threshold, entities: chunk }])));
acceptanceCriteria: FuzzySearch uses workerpool.pool() instead of custom WorkerPool

stepByStep: Remove or comment out WorkerPool export
Modify tests to use workerpool API (pool.exec, pool.stats, pool.terminate)
Ensure all tests still validate expected behavior
Option B: Add @deprecated JSDoc and keep for reference
Update Phase 7 Parallel Fuzzy Search section to reference workerpool
Update workers/ module description if WorkerPool.ts is removed

notes: This sprint focuses on a drop-in replacement of the custom WorkerPool with workerpool. The API is similar enough that the migration should be straightforward. The main changes are: (1) worker registration via workerpool.worker(), (2) task execution via pool.exec(), and (3) pool termination via pool.terminate().
